# Painting { #tspaint.Painting }

```python
tspaint.Painting(
    posteriors,
    Q,
    pi,
    w,
    loglik_history,
    queries,
    ts=None,
    labels=None,
    default_deadband=0.0,
    n_jobs=1,
    _seqlen=None,
    _member_posteriors=None,
)
```

Result of :func:`paint`: the soft local-ancestry posteriors plus the fitted model.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**posteriors**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[Segment](`tspaint.output.Segment`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per query haplotype, the down-pass posterior over ancestry states as contiguous :class:`~tspaint.output.Segment`\ s covering ``[0, L)`` (the soft, calibrated deliverable). When :func:`paint` was given an **ensemble** of tree sequences these are :class:`~tspaint.ensemble.MergedSegment`\ s â€” the ensemble-mean posterior plus a ``posterior_std`` ARG-uncertainty band â€” but otherwise behave identically (same ``left``/``right``/``posterior``/``status``).

<code>[**Q**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The fitted generator.

<code>[**pi**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The fitted root frequencies.

<code>[**w**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The learned per-tip credibility.

<code>[**loglik_history**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Observed-data log-likelihood per EM step (non-decreasing).

<code>[**queries**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The painted sample-node ids.

<code>[**ts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`) or [list](`list`)\[[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The tree sequence painted â€” or the ensemble of them â€” retained so :meth:`introgression_map` / :meth:`rate_through_time` can reuse the fit (already in memory, so no extra cost).

<code>[**labels**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The reference labels used for the fit (retained for :meth:`rate_through_time`).

<code>[**default_deadband**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Default dead-band passed to :meth:`segments`. Default ``0.0``.

<code>[**_member_posteriors**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[dict](`dict`)\] or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   For an **ensemble** painting, the individual per-member posterior tables (each a ``dict[int, list[Segment]]``) whose per-position mean is :attr:`posteriors`; ``None`` for a single tree sequence. Drives the per-member :meth:`rate_through_time` split-time interval.

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [introgression_map](#tspaint.Painting.introgression_map) | Leave-one-out introgression map for ``sample``, reusing this painting's fit. |
| [load](#tspaint.Painting.load) | Reload a painting written by :meth:`save` (``ts`` is ``None``; see :meth:`save`). |
| [plot](#tspaint.Painting.plot) | Stacked strip plot of the painting â€” one row per query haplotype. |
| [posterior_at](#tspaint.Painting.posterior_at) | Posterior vector for ``sample`` at a genomic ``position``. |
| [rate_through_time](#tspaint.Painting.rate_through_time) | Estimate the admixture (cross-ancestry) rate through time, reusing this fit. |
| [save](#tspaint.Painting.save) | Write this painting to ``path`` as ``.npz`` (the segment table plus the fitted model). |
| [segments](#tspaint.Painting.segments) | Hard ancestry segments for downstream tract-length / dating analysis. |

### introgression_map { #tspaint.Painting.introgression_map }

```python
tspaint.Painting.introgression_map(sample)
```

Leave-one-out introgression map for ``sample``, reusing this painting's fit.

Returns what the rest of the genealogy says about ``sample`` *excluding its own
emission* (:func:`tspaint.output.loo_posterior_table`). Unlike :attr:`posteriors` (the
down-pass), it is not suppressed by a confident tip emission, so it surfaces a labelled
reference's own foreign tracts â€” the reference-introgression / mislabel lens
(CLAUDE.md Â§2.3, Â§9). For a panel-wide audit see :func:`tspaint.reference_qc`.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**sample**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sample-node id to map (a labelled reference or a query).

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[tspaint](`tspaint`).[output](`tspaint.output`).[Segment](`tspaint.output.Segment`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The leave-one-out posterior as contiguous segments covering ``[0, L)`` (for an ensemble painting, the ensemble-mean leave-one-out map as :class:`~tspaint.ensemble.MergedSegment`\ s).

### load { #tspaint.Painting.load }

```python
tspaint.Painting.load(path)
```

Reload a painting written by :meth:`save` (``ts`` is ``None``; see :meth:`save`).

### plot { #tspaint.Painting.plot }

```python
tspaint.Painting.plot(
    truth=None,
    title=None,
    cmap='coolwarm',
    colors=None,
    return_plot=False,
)
```

Stacked strip plot of the painting â€” one row per query haplotype.

Each haplotype row shows, top to bottom: the **soft** per-position posterior
``P(ancestry A)`` as a colour gradient (the calibrated deliverable), the **hard**
ancestry segments (:meth:`segments` at ``deadband=0.4``), and â€” when ``truth`` is given â€”
the true ancestry tracts as a reference track beneath. A shared colour bar maps
``P(ancestry A)`` (state A at the top of ``cmap``, state B at the bottom). Works for an
ensemble painting too (the rows show the ensemble-mean posterior). Requires matplotlib.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**truth**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Ground-truth ancestry tracts ``(left, right, state)`` per query â€” e.g. from :func:`tspaint.local_ancestry_truth` mapped to states ``0``/``1`` â€” drawn as a reference track below each haplotype. No truth track is drawn when ``None`` (default).

<code>[**title**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Title placed on the top haplotype axes.

<code>[**cmap**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`) or [matplotlib](`matplotlib`).[colors](`matplotlib.colors`).[Colormap](`matplotlib.colors.Colormap`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [\'coolwarm\']{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Diverging colormap mapping ``P(ancestry A) âˆˆ [0, 1]`` (state A â†’ ``1.0``, state B â†’ ``0.0``). Default ``'coolwarm'``. Ignored when ``colors`` is given.

<code>[**colors**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Colours to build a custom diverging colormap from, overriding ``cmap`` â€” e.g. ``["#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"]``.

<code>[**return_plot**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [False]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Return the Matplotlib ``(figure, axes)`` so the caller can further customise or save the plot, instead of ``None``. Default ``False``.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tuple](`tuple`) or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``(figure, list_of_axes)`` if ``return_plot`` is ``True``, else ``None``.

### posterior_at { #tspaint.Painting.posterior_at }

```python
tspaint.Painting.posterior_at(sample, position)
```

Posterior vector for ``sample`` at a genomic ``position``.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**sample**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sample-node id to look up.

<code>[**position**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Genomic position.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The ``(K,)`` posterior covering ``position``, or ``None`` if uncovered.

### rate_through_time { #tspaint.Painting.rate_through_time }

```python
tspaint.Painting.rate_through_time(edges=None, *, n_jobs=None, **kwargs)
```

Estimate the admixture (cross-ancestry) rate through time, reusing this fit.

Fits the time-inhomogeneous directional mugration EM
(:func:`tspaint.fit_rate_through_time`) **warm-started from this painting's fitted
``(Q, Ï€, w)``**, so the homogeneous fit is not repeated. This is a *different
deliverable* from the painting â€” the cross-ancestry transition rates ``q_AB(t)``,
``q_BA(t)`` as functions of (backward) time, locating divergence / gene-flow epochs and
their direction. It returns a **new** :class:`~tspaint.dating.RateThroughTime` and does
**not** modify :attr:`posteriors` (CLAUDE.md: Q(t) gives no painting-accuracy gain, so the
paths stay side by side).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**edges**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[array_like](`array_like`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Log-time grid edges; an auto grid is built from the node ages when ``None``.

<code>[**n_jobs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   For an **ensemble** painting, worker processes dating the members **in parallel** (one per member â€” they are independent). Defaults to the painting's own :attr:`n_jobs` (so a painting fit in parallel dates in parallel); pass an explicit value to override. Ignored for a single tree sequence (the dating E-step is not yet tree-parallel).

<code>[****kwargs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [{}]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Forwarded to :func:`tspaint.fit_rate_through_time` (e.g. ``n_cells``, ``n_iter``, ``n_knots``).

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tspaint](`tspaint`).[dating](`tspaint.dating`).[RateThroughTime](`tspaint.dating.RateThroughTime`) or [tspaint](`tspaint`).[dating](`tspaint.dating`).[EnsembleRateThroughTime](`tspaint.dating.EnsembleRateThroughTime`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   For a single tree sequence, the directional rate-through-time profile (``.centers``, ``.q_AB``, ``.q_BA``, ``.plot()``). For an **ensemble** painting, an :class:`~tspaint.dating.EnsembleRateThroughTime`: every member is dated on the shared fit and grid, and the per-member split-time estimates form a **confidence interval on the split time** (``.split_time()``, ``.split_time_ci()``) â€” the ARG-uncertainty band on *when* the ancestries diverged.

### save { #tspaint.Painting.save }

```python
tspaint.Painting.save(path)
```

Write this painting to ``path`` as ``.npz`` (the segment table plus the fitted model).

Reload with :meth:`load`. The painted tree sequence itself is **not** stored (keep the
``.trees`` file); a reloaded painting therefore has ``ts=None`` but retains
:attr:`length`, the posteriors, ``Q``/``Ï€``/``w`` and the labels.

### segments { #tspaint.Painting.segments }

```python
tspaint.Painting.segments(deadband=None)
```

Hard ancestry segments for downstream tract-length / dating analysis.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**deadband**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Confidence dead-band suppressing low-confidence flips that fragment long tracts. Defaults to :attr:`default_deadband`. See :func:`tspaint.output.hard_segments`.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per query, hard ``(left, right, state)`` segments.
